title: SAF Drones Strike Sarf Umra Market and Government HQ, North Darfur; Civilian Casualties
detail: SAF drones struck Sarf Umra (also spelled Saraf Omra), North Darfur, hitting the local government headquarters, a fuel tanker, and the main market. Eyewitness Azzaldin Ali described explosions heard across the area with 'significant human and material losses, several people killed or injured and more than six shops destroyed.' The RSF condemned the attack alongside the Al Daein hospital strike as part of what it characterized as 'systematic bombing operations against civilians.' The exact casualty count was still being compiled, but local sources reported multiple deaths and injuries. The strike followed a wave of SAF drone attacks on RSF-held population centers across Darfur and Kordofan in late March 2026.

media.caption: SAF drones strike Sarf Umra market and government HQ in North Darfur

title: RSF-SPLM-N Alliance Advances Toward Ed Damazin After Kurmuk Fall; Roseires Dam at Risk
detail: Three days after capturing Kurmuk, the RSF-SPLM-N alliance under commander Joseph Touka expanded operations south, east, and west of Ed Damazin in Blue Nile State. Forces pushed toward the Geissan district on the Ethiopian border, approximately 200 km from Ed Damazin. Regional government sources warned that Geissan was at imminent risk of falling, which would directly threaten Ed Damazin, Blue Nile's state capital, and the Roseires Dam — Sudan's second-largest hydroelectric facility providing power to much of central and eastern Sudan. Approximately 3,000 more civilians, mostly women and children, fled toward Ed Damazin and the Ethiopian border as fighting expanded. The loss of the Roseires Dam would be catastrophic for Sudan's already devastated power infrastructure.

media.caption: RSF-SPLM-N alliance advances toward Ed Damazin after Kurmuk fall

title: SAF Drone Strikes Civilian Vehicle in Al-Rahad, North Kordofan — 6 Killed, 12 Injured
detail: A drone strike attributed to SAF hit a civilian passenger vehicle heading toward Um Rawaba in Al-Rahad (Al-Rahad), North Kordofan on March 26. Eyewitnesses reported 6 people killed and 12 injured. The attack occurred on the same day as the Sarf Umra market strike, part of a wave of drone attacks across Darfur and Kordofan that killed 28 and wounded 29 in the two incidents combined (Xinhua). Al-Rahad is in RSF-contested North Kordofan, near SAF supply lines between El Obeid and northern Kordofan.

media.caption: SAF drone strikes civilian vehicle in Al-Rahad, North Kordofan

title: New UN Envoy Haavisto Meets Burhan in Port Sudan; No Ceasefire Agreement
detail: UN Secretary-General's newly appointed Personal Envoy for Sudan, Finnish diplomat Pekka Haavisto, met with SAF commander and Sovereignty Council Chairman Lt. Gen. Abdel Fattah al-Burhan in Port Sudan on his first visit since replacing Ramtane Lamamra in late February 2026. Haavisto reaffirmed UN commitment to facilitating ceasefire negotiations and civilian protection. No concrete agreements were reached. Al-Burhan has previously stated Sudan will not accept a ceasefire 'so long as the rebel militia remains on any inch of the country.' The visit occurred against a backdrop of escalating drone warfare and the collapse of Blue Nile's Kurmuk to the RSF-SPLM-N alliance three days prior.

media.caption: New UN envoy Haavisto meets Burhan in Port Sudan but no ceasefire agreement reached
